text:
Once upon a time in the bustling town of Willowbrook, there was an old tree known only as Sir Oakley. He wasn't tall—or broad—with leaves; he was just an oak. Yet, every autumn, Willowbrook would whisper tales of how he nurtured every little squirrel with his bounty of acorns.

The squirrels, in turn, loved Sir Oakley. The bravest among them was Brighteyes. Her little world revolved around nuts. One year, though, despite filling her hoard to the brim, she discovered something wasn't right.

She noticed a strange shadow falling upon her favorite branches every night. With a wise old heart, she knew this wasn't normal. That's when she discovered the hole.

It wasn't a squirrel—it was a gnome. But not just any gnome. It was Sir Gnomenoire, the guardian of autumn treasures. With a twinkling eye that sparkled with mischief, he had snatched an enchanted acorn.

G
